blocks.content: She met me there. Right at the edge of the decision. Her hand came up to my arm, light, just for a second. And we were close enough that the city noise went somewhere else.
It wasn't long. It didn't need to be. She pulled back first and looked at me with something I couldn't name.
She turned left. I walked right. My heart didn't slow down until the next block.
She smiled. Turned. I stood there on the corner and watched her go. Half a block before she was too far. Something in the way she walked — unhurried, like she'd gotten what she came for.
I walked home thinking about the space between what almost happened and what didn't.
The city was doing its thing around me. I barely noticed it.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: She stayed for like twenty minutes after practice.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: She showed me what she wrote. About the trust thing. Between a QB and a receiver.
He said it like someone who'd figured something out and was being careful with it. Like he didn't want to break it by saying it too loud.
I understood that. I walked home with it sitting with me — the thing Jonathan was feeling, and the shape of my own week, and how both of us were standing at some edge we hadn't crossed yet.
